text: Handling deadlines effectively is a crucial skill in both work and study. Good time management starts with breaking large tasks into smaller steps and scheduling them in a calendar. Prioritization helps you decide which tasks are most urgent or important, so you focus on what truly matters first. Scope management means knowing when to say "no" to extra requests that could overwhelm your schedule; it’s about keeping your work realistic. Finally, clear communication with your team or manager is essential—if you foresee a delay, let them know early. By combining these strategies, you can meet your deadlines with less stress and better results.
